bounded_document_family: FDA warning letters with numbered CGMP violation items and firm-response critiques.
recurring_anatomy: FDA states whether the firm's response or corrective action is adequate for a numbered CGMP item.
FDA states that supporting documentation, protocols, records, or validation evidence were not provided.
FDA states that a discrepancy or contamination event was not investigated or not thoroughly investigated.

argument_roles.name: assessment_id
argument_roles.purpose: Stable local id for one response-assessment item.

argument_roles.name: violation_id
argument_roles.purpose: Existing local violation_N atom from the numbered CGMP item map.

argument_roles.name: cgmps_citation
argument_roles.purpose: Compact CGMP citation family addressed by the response critique.

argument_roles.name: assessment_kind
argument_roles.purpose: Closed compact judgment about the response or corrective action.

argument_roles.name: assessment_scope
argument_roles.purpose: Closed compact purpose of the assessment.

argument_roles.name: source_or_scope
argument_roles.purpose: Source coordinate or governed scope atom.

forbidden_examples.fact: fda_response_assessment_item(bad_quote, violation_1, cfr_21_211_113_b, response_inadequate, response_review, full_response_paragraph).

unlike_transfer_plan.heldout_policy: unlike FDA warning letters not used to shape the response-assessment carrier or value domains

abstention_boundary: Do not emit when the critique cannot be attached to an existing typed violation_N item and matching CGMP citation family.
Do not emit when the needed value is a source-specific missing-document title, organism name, firm's explanation, or critique sentence.
Do not use this carrier to solve wrapper-role semantics, violation category projection, or open violation-detail value questions.
Do not promote a score lift unless final projected rows survive redaction replay and typed-plan replay.
